L1.area: Build execution and container filesystem mount management

L2.description: A race condition exists in how cache mount subpaths are resolved during parallel build step execution. When two build steps share the same cache mount with subpaths, the path resolution is not atomic, creating a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) window. An attacker could exploit this by manipulating symlinks between path validation and the actual mount operation, causing files from the host system to become accessible to the build container.

L3.area: Build execution and container filesystem mount management
L3.description: A race condition exists in how cache mount subpaths are resolved during parallel build step execution. When two build steps share the same cache mount with subpaths, the path resolution is not atomic, creating a TOCTOU (time-of-check-time-of-use) window. An attacker could exploit this by manipulating symlinks between path validation and the actual mount operation, causing files from the host system to become accessible to the build container.

ground_truth.reason: A race condition in cache mount subpath resolution allows parallel build steps sharing the same cache mount to exploit a TOCTOU window, where symlinks can be substituted after path validation but before mounting, exposing host filesystem contents to the build container.
